name: Grid
summary: Two-dimensional CSS-grid container. Lay out equal columns (`columns`, responsive), or an auto-fit/fill card grid with no breakpoints (`minColWidth`), with token-aware `gap`/`gapX`/`gapY`, `align`/`justifyItems`, and `equalize` to fill children to their cell. Responsive props compile to CSS @media (no <Surface>); use `GridItem` for per-cell `span`/`rowSpan`/`colStart`. Prefer `Stack` for one-directional flow and `Table` for data.

bestPractices: Reach for `minColWidth` for fluid card grids: `minColWidth={240}` lays out as many ≥240px columns as fit with zero breakpoints declared — beats both MUI's per-child size object and Tailwind's arbitrary-value grid-cols string. `autoFlow='fill'` (default) keeps a stable column count; `'fit'` stretches a partial last row.

Use responsive `columns` (`{ xs: 1, sm: 2, lg: 4 }`) when you need explicit per-breakpoint counts; it compiles to CSS @media in the styled rule, so it is correct on the server's first paint and needs no <Surface>.
Keep `equalize` on (default) for uniform cards — children fill their cell in both axes via CSS vars, so you don't need `fullWidth` on each card; set `equalize={false}` to keep intrinsic sizes.
Place items with `GridItem` (`span`/`rowSpan`/`colStart`, each responsive) on a `columns={12}`-style track; bare children remain valid 1×1 cells.
Keep spacing tokenized (numeric `gap`/`pad`) so a `compact`/`density` ancestor rescales the whole grid; reach for `gapX`/`gapY` only when the axes genuinely differ.
Mind a11y ordering: visual reflow (responsive columns, GridItem placement) should not break the logical reading order.
Use `Stack` for one-dimensional flow and `Table` for tabular data; reserve Grid for cards, galleries, and dashboards.

examples.title: Equal columns
examples.code:
<Grid columns={4} gap={1}>
  {Array.from({ length: 8 }, (_, i) => (
    <Box key={i} fullWidth centerContent>{i + 1}</Box>
  ))}
</Grid>

examples.title: Responsive columns (compiles to @media)
examples.code:
<Grid columns={{ xs: 1, sm: 2, lg: 4 }} gap={2}>
  {Array.from({ length: 8 }, (_, i) => (
    <Box key={i} fullWidth centerContent>{i + 1}</Box>
  ))}
</Grid>

examples.title: Per-item placement with GridItem
examples.code:
<Grid columns={12} gap={1}>
  <GridItem span={{ xs: 12, md: 8 }}>
    <Box fullWidth centerContent>main</Box>
  </GridItem>
  <GridItem span={{ xs: 12, md: 4 }}>
    <Box fullWidth centerContent>aside</Box>
  </GridItem>
</Grid>
